transcript:
Agent: Records department, what do you need?
Caller: Hi, I'm calling to request my medical records.
Agent: Okay, what's your name?
Caller: Lisa Chen.
Agent: Alright Lisa, what records do you need?
Caller: I need my complete treatment history from last year sent to my new doctor.
Agent: Yeah sure, I'll send everything over. What's the fax number for your new doctor?
Caller: It's 555-0192. But don't you need to verify my identity first or have me sign something?
Agent: Nah, we have your name, that's enough. I'll fax it over today.
Caller: Are you sure? My previous clinic always required a signed authorization.
Agent: Look, do you want the records or not? I'm trying to help you here. We do this all the time, it's fine.
Caller: Okay... I guess. Can you also tell me what my diagnosis was?
Agent: Yeah, it says here you have lumbar radiculopathy and you were on oxycodone 40mg. I can see your whole chart.
Caller: Oh, um, I didn't expect you to just read that out like that.
Agent: You asked. Anything else?
Caller: No, I think that's it.
Agent: Okay, bye.
